summary: Excellent matrix recording of the historic April 29 1971 Fillmore East show, highlighted by a superb Me & My Uncle, Bird Song, Alligator and early‑era Dark Hollow performances.
review: Fans rave about this almost‑perfect matrix capture of the Dead’s final night at the Fillmore East, calling it one of the best‑sounding releases from that era. The performance shines with standout renditions of Me & My Uncle, Bird Song, Alligator (the last‑ever version) and a tight Dark Hollow, while Phil Lesh’s bass work earns particular praise. Overall the set flows beautifully through two strong halves, making it a must‑have for any Deadhead collection.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviews are five‑star and uniformly praise both the musical performance and the matrix recording quality; the show is described as historic and superb, while the source is called “best kind” matrix with only minor imperfections.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note the sound is almost perfect, describing it as a top‑tier matrix capture; one mentions it isn’t quite as pristine as the commercial CD but still “better than what I deserve.” No significant dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds with early‑era gems and solid rockers, while the second opens strong (“Great Dew”) and delivers a memorable Alligator finale; reviewers emphasize the historic context of the final Fillmore East night, noting an excellent overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his pedal‑steel intro with the New Riders and generally solid guitar work throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass playing alone is highlighted as a five‑star reason; reviewers call it “awesome.”
